summary: Main-character scouting found strong CC0 animation fallback packs, but no free distinctive mascot fit; Meshy generation was justified for the lead hero identity.
observations: Official Kenney source lists Blaster Kit 2.1 as Creative Commons CC0.
The kit includes GLB models and preview images suitable for a quick first-person weapon selector.
Only blaster-a, blaster-h, and blaster-p were imported for the first payload.
KayKit Adventurers plus KayKit Character Animations is the best free practical humanoid animation fallback.
Quaternius Ultimate/Base Characters plus Universal Animation Library is the safest CC0 retargeting/reference fallback.
No free source matched the requested distinctive playful lead mascot without substantial customization.
decisions: Use Kenney Blaster Kit 2.1 for the first weapon slice.
Defer larger gun-pack imports until a level-specific roster needs them.
Keep the external-source gate CC0-only for this early mobile prototype.
Use free CC0 packs as fallback animation/retargeting references only.
Proceed with Meshy/custom generation for original main-character identity.
caughtIssues: No enemy or environment asset brief exists yet, so weapon selection stayed generic sci-fi.
Mixamo has useful development animations but raw redistribution is not a good fit for this repo.
Orange fox-like concepts need originality review to avoid established mascot similarity.
risks: The stylized sci-fi look may need art-direction review once the first level theme is set.

fallbackSources: Quaternius Base/Ultimate Characters + Universal Animation Library
